title: ADHD scope creep safeguards: hard stops prevent 20hr hyperflow sessions
description: Developer with ADHD is vulnerable to infinite iteration feedback loops when working with AI agents. Previous session resulted in 20hr hyperflow and a 137-file PR at 3AM. Safeguards needed to enforce boundaries.
root_cause: AI agent interactions create dopamine-driven feedback loops: each small win encourages the next iteration. Without external brakes, the developer-agent pair will optimize indefinitely.
solution: Implement session boundaries: visible progress tracking, decision gates ('stop here and ship?'), periodic checkpoints (~45min), hard stops at 2-3hr or 20+ files changed. Make small shipped features feel like wins.
lesson_summary: Build scope creep safeguards into the agent workflow itself. The agent should actively propose stopping points rather than waiting for the user to disengage. Small shipped PRs > giant hyperflow PRs.

code_pattern.checkpoint: Every ~45min: show accomplishments vs out-of-scope items
code_pattern.decision_gate: Before expanding: 'Stop here and ship? Or continue with X?'
code_pattern.hard_stop: At 2-3hr or 20+ files: suggest commit and defer remaining work

implementation.next: Add checkpoint logic to session management
